item.title: Halkitis Resignation Calls, Davis Silent
item.ns0:title: PM Maintains Silence on Bahamian Detained in U.S. Drug Case and Alleged Political Ties — Monday, June 1st, 2026
item.description:
Tonight on Our News, Prime Minister Philip Davis remains silent as questions intensify regarding a Bahamian man detained in the United States and his alleged connections to a local politician. We examine the growing calls for the Finance Minister’s resignation and provide essential preparedness updates as the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season officially gets underway. Our team also takes you inside the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting at Baha Mar and brings you the latest on a tragic discovery in a western gated community. Stay tuned for these stories and a heartwarming community effort to support special needs education.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tonight on Our News, Prime Minister Philip Davis remains silent as questions intensify regarding a Bahamian man detained in the United States and his alleged connections to a local politician. We examine the growing calls for the Finance Minister’s resignation and provide essential preparedness updates as the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season officially gets underway. Our team also takes you inside the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting at Baha Mar and brings you the latest on a tragic discovery in a western gated community. Stay tuned for these stories and a heartwarming community effort to support special needs education.

item.ns0:summary:
Tonight on Our News, Prime Minister Philip Davis remains silent as questions intensify regarding a Bahamian man detained in the United States and his alleged connections to a local politician. We examine the growing calls for the Finance Minister’s resignation and provide essential preparedness updates as the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season officially gets underway. Our team also takes you inside the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting at Baha Mar and brings you the latest on a tragic discovery in a western gated community. Stay tuned for these stories and a heartwarming community effort to support special needs education.

item.ns0:author: Our News Bahamas
